MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: DC growth holding ~+66–68% YoY; customer concentration formally disclosed at 4-way >10%; China is now effectively zero, but supply/energy commentary unchanged.

shifts.note: Slight re-acceleration on a full-year basis after Blackwell ramp
shifts.trend: DC YoY +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year); Networking +162% on GB200/GB300 NVLink ramp

shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependence newly visible — diligence risk-additive
shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers each >10% of revenue (22/15/13/11%); 10-K did not previously break this out

shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired across both filings
shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0B vs $8.1B prior-year Q3; H20 effectively zero by FY26 after $4.5B Q1 inventory charge; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
Non-GAAP GM 73.4% (Q3) → 73.5% guide (Q1 FY27); compressed YoY on Blackwell full-system mix but holding within target

shifts.note: Quote nearly identical across the two filings
shifts.trend: Both filings cite data-center / energy / capital availability as the gating constraint on customer deployments — no easing language added

shifts.note: Vendor-financing footprint growing — watch FCF translation
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K disclosed $17.5B in private/infra investments plus $3.5B land/power/shell guarantees to early-stage AI customers; Q3 added $7.5B new DC lease obligations FY26 Q4–FY30

NVDA.verdict: Story still about supply-constrained AI capex. The new disclosures (4-way customer concentration, vendor-financing footprint) are risk-additive but don't break the thesis; China optionality is now fully impaired and should be modeled at zero.

AMAT.headline: China collapsed 16% and top-line growth decelerated to -2% YoY in Q1; DRAM/HBM re-rated the mix as leading-edge logic softened.

shifts.trend: FY25 +4% → Q1 FY26 -2% YoY
shifts.note: HBM demand partially offsets weaker trailing-edge logic

shifts.trend: DRAM mix in SemiSystems: 26% FY25 → 34% Q1 FY26; foundry/logic fell from 67% to 62%
shifts.note: Memory taking share of capex from foundry

shifts.trend: China revenue $8.5B (-16%) FY25 → $2.1B (-7%) Q1 FY26; as % of total 30% FY25 → 30% Q1 FY26 (held via rebound)
shifts.note: $253M BIS compliance settlement booked in Q1 FY26 for prior-period violations

shifts.trend: Gross margin 48.7% FY25 → 49.0% Q1 FY26; operating margin 30.4% FY25 → 26.1% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Operating margin dip mostly from one-time $253M legal settlement; underlying relatively stable

AMAT.verdict: Thesis intact but watch: leading-edge logic capex is weakening, and China settlement risk remains unresolved. HBM/DRAM mix shift is the support.

AMD.headline: Data Center revenue re-accelerated sharply to +57% YoY in Q1 FY26, DC margin expanded to 28%, and gross margin crossed 53% — the China export-control headwind has become a licensing negotiation rather than a hard stop.

shifts.trend: DC YoY: FY25 +32% → Q1 FY26 +57%; total revenue FY25 +34% → Q1 FY26 +38%
shifts.note: OpenAI 6 GW deal (Oct 2025) and MI350 ramp both contributing

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 50% FY25 → 53% Q1 FY26; DC op margin: 22% FY25 → 28% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Richer DC mix driving improvement; DC still below NVDA's levels but trending up

shifts.trend: FY25 MI308 net charge ~$440M → Q1 FY26 MI325 licenses granted with 25% re-import tariff
shifts.note: Partial resolution — China channel reopened at added cost but not closed

shifts.trend: No customer >10% FY25; Q1 FY26 international sales 74% (vs 66% prior year) as hyperscaler GPU shipments dominate
shifts.note: No single customer disclosed but hyperscaler dependency rising

AMD.verdict: DC re-acceleration unlocks the story. If MI450 ramp at OpenAI executes, AMD is in a structurally multi-year DC GPU cycle — not just catching up to NVDA.

AMKR.headline: Net sales accelerated to +27% YoY in Q1 with gross margin expanding 230bps, while 2026 capex guidance of $2.5-3.0B (3x FY25) was reaffirmed — the Arizona advanced-packaging buildout is the multi-year investment thesis.

shifts.note: All four end markets now growing vs mixed in FY25

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 14.0% FY25 → 14.2% Q1 26 (+2.3pts YoY in Q1); op margin: 7.0% FY25 improving
shifts.note: Higher factory utilization driving improvement as Arizona construction begins

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $904.6M → Q1 2026 $224.6M (vs $79.9M Q1 25); FY26 guidance $2.5-3.0B reaffirmed
shifts.note: Biggest capex ramp in company history for Arizona FCBGA/2.5D/HBM facility

shifts.trend: Apple 29.8%, Qualcomm 11.1% in FY25; Q1 26 concentration unchanged at top-10 = 72%
shifts.note: Apple dependency is the key single-customer risk

AMKR.verdict: Thesis intact: Arizona is the multi-year capex and growth driver. Near-term execution risk is the ramp curve from $0.9B to $3B capex in one year.

AMZN.headline: AWS re-accelerated to +28% with RPO nearly doubling to $364B in one quarter; Q1 2026 capex hit $43.2B (78% YoY increase) and 2026 capex is guided to increase further.

shifts.trend: AWS YoY: +20% FY25 → +28% Q1 2026; AWS RPO: $244B at YE25 → $364B at Q1 26
shifts.note: $100B OpenAI expansion and $50B direct Amazon commitment to OpenAI driving RPO jump
Management explicitly guided 2026 total to exceed FY25's $128.3B

shifts.trend: AWS operating margin: 35.4% FY25 → 37.7% Q1 2026 (vs 39.4% prior-year Q1)
shifts.note: Margin slightly below prior year on AI infra investment mix

shifts.trend: OpenAI: $38B existing + $100B expansion; Amazon direct: $50B committed; Anthropic: $5B+ and $20B compute facility
shifts.note: AI model company commitments dominating RPO growth

AMZN.verdict: Demand signal intact and strengthening — RPO surge to $364B in one quarter is unprecedented. Capex acceleration raises execution risk but validates AWS as the primary AI infrastructure anchor.

ANET.headline: Revenue growth accelerated from +29% in FY25 to +35% in Q1 FY26; deferred revenue jumped $826M in one quarter to $6.20B as AI Ethernet acceptance contracts accumulate, but gross margin compressed 180bps on large-customer mix.

shifts.note: AI Ethernet ramp (EtherLink 800G/400G) converting trial orders to revenue

shifts.trend: Deferred revenue: $2.79B YE24 → $5.37B YE25 → $6.20B Q1 FY26; RPO $7.7B at Q1 end
shifts.note: Acceptance-period AI Ethernet contracts keep building; memory/silicon supply flagged as gating

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 64.1% FY25 → 61.9% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Large-customer volume pricing and memory procurement costs pressuring GM

shifts.trend: Cloud+AI Titans 48% FY25 → Americas 84.5% Q1 26 (vs 79.7%); top-2 at 26% and 16%
shifts.note: US-based hyperscaler/AI titan concentration rising steadily

ANET.verdict: Thesis intact. The deferred revenue accumulation and 35% growth confirm ANET is the primary AI Ethernet switching beneficiary. Gross margin compression is the new risk to monitor.

AVGO.headline: AI XPU demand re-concentrated: top-5 customers jumped from 40% to 50% of revenue and one distributor hit 42% in Q1, while semiconductor solutions grew +52% YoY on custom accelerators and AI networking.

shifts.trend: Semiconductor solutions: FY25 +22% → Q1 FY26 +52% YoY
shifts.note: Custom AI XPU and AI networking the primary drivers; software (VMware) growth slowed to +1% Q1

shifts.trend: Top-5 customers: ~40% FY25 → ~50% Q1 FY26; one distributor: 28% FY24 → 32% FY25 → 42% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Extreme concentration in one hyperscaler XPU customer — biggest single-customer risk in the coverage

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 68% FY25 → 68% Q1 FY26 (flat)
shifts.note: Higher semi mix vs software negative but AI XPU pricing offset; AI rack-leasing model flagged as future GM headwind

shifts.trend: China shipments: 20% FY24 → 17% FY25; end-customer China exposure substantially lower
shifts.note: Less China-sensitive than peers; mostly distributor routing

AVGO.verdict: XPU concentration in one hyperscaler is the primary new risk — 42% of quarterly revenue through one distributor is extreme. Story intact if that customer stays committed; very binary.

CAT.headline: Backlog surged from $30B to $51B in FY25, and Power & Energy revenue accelerated to +22% YoY in Q1 FY26 — prime power demand from data centers remains the primary demand driver even as tariffs pressure margins.

shifts.trend: P&E revenue: FY25 +12% → Q1 FY26 +22% YoY; backlog: $30.0B YE24 → $51.2B YE25 → ongoing
shifts.note: Power Generation sub-segment (data center prime power) the lead driver

shifts.trend: P&E margin: 19.9% FY25 → 17.7% consolidated Q1; tariffs adding ~$700M headwind in Q2
shifts.note: Tariff pressure (IEEPA) is the margin risk; SCOTUS ruled IEEPA unauthorized but refund not yet deemed probable

shifts.trend: Backlog $51.2B at YE25 (vs $30.0B YE24); $19.3B of backlog not expected to fill in 2026
shifts.note: Multi-year visibility — backlog build well ahead of delivery capacity

shifts.trend: FY26 guidance: low double-digit growth, consistent between 10-K and Q1 10-Q
shifts.note: Tariff cost guidance $2.2-2.4B maintained

CAT.verdict: Thesis intact. Backlog visibility to 2026-2027 and prime power commentary re-confirmed gives high conviction. Tariff refund risk is worth monitoring but doesn't change the demand picture.

CDNS.headline: Growth re-accelerated from +14% FY25 to +19% Q1 FY26; China revenue actually grew +36% YoY as BIS export-control whiplash resolved; RPO backlog expanded from $7.8B to $8.0B.

shifts.note: Hardware/up-front mix growing; Hexagon D&E acquisition closing

shifts.trend: China revenue: FY25 +19% to $680M (13%) → Q1 FY26 +36% to $189M (13%)
shifts.note: BIS 'is-informed' letter (May 2025) rescinded July 2025; BIS 50%-affiliate rule still an overhang

shifts.trend: RPO: $7.8B YE25 → $8.0B Q1 FY26; cRPO share: 53% → 55% in next 12 months
shifts.note: Steady backlog build; near-term visibility increasing

shifts.trend: Operating margin: 28% FY25 (impacted by $128.5M BIS/DOJ settlement) → 29% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: FY25 margin was impacted by the one-time settlement charge; underlying closer to 29%

CDNS.verdict: Thesis intact. China revenue growing despite trade overhang is an unexpected positive. Hexagon D&E is the next complexity-design opportunity tied to AI chip simulation.

CEG.headline: Calpine closed Jan 2026 for ~$22B transforming CEG into the world's largest private power producer at 55 GW; nuclear PPAs with hyperscalers represent the long-duration demand anchor.

shifts.trend: Operating revenues: $23.6B FY24 → $25.5B FY25 (+8%); post-Calpine run-rate materially higher
shifts.note: Calpine adds 27 GW; full first-year contribution will show in FY26 10-K

shifts.trend: 20-year PPA with Meta (Clinton), 20-year PPA with Microsoft (Crane/TMI 1); Crane DOE $1B loan guarantee Nov 2025
shifts.note: Nuclear contracted directly to AI hyperscalers — first such deals in commercial nuclear history

shifts.trend: Calpine acquisition ~$22B; Crane restart capital + $1B DOE loan; NY ZEC through 2049
shifts.note: Capital deployment toward nuclear expansion unprecedented in recent nuclear history

shifts.trend: Adjusted operating EPS: $8.67 FY24 → $9.39 FY25; GAAP net income lower on reduced nuclear PTC
shifts.note: Nuclear PTC fluctuates with market prices; adj EPS better signal

CEG.verdict: Thesis significantly unlocked. Calpine close + hyperscaler nuclear PPA model positions CEG as the only public company with multi-decade direct contracted nuclear supply to AI data centers.

CLS.headline: HPS revenue growth decelerated from +81% FY25 to +63% Q1 FY26 but from a larger base; Enterprise rebooted at +101% Q1 on next-gen AI/ML compute ramp; revolver upsized to $1.75B to fund the build-out.

shifts.note: HPS YoY rate naturally decelerating from very high base; absolute dollars still accelerating

shifts.trend: Enterprise end-market: FY25 -19% (program pause) → Q1 FY26 +101% (next-gen ramp)
shifts.note: Paused compute program restarted on next-gen MI450/Blackwell cycle — adds incremental to existing switch programs

shifts.trend: 3 CCS customers each >10% in FY25 (32%, 14%, 12%); Q1 26 CCS = 80% of total revenue
shifts.note: Three hyperscaler customers = >55% of total company revenue — extreme concentration risk

shifts.trend: FY26 capex guided ~$1B (6% of revenue) vs historical 1.5-2.0%; revolver $750M → $1.75B
shifts.note: Balance sheet expanding to fund hyperscaler capacity commitments

CLS.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening: Enterprise program restart adds another growth vector. Customer concentration is the primary thesis risk — one hyperscaler pause equals ~15% revenue shock.

CMI.headline: Power Systems EBITDA margin expanded from 22.7% in FY25 to 29.5% in Q1 FY26 as data-center-driven power generation demand accelerates; 6-8 quarter backlog commentary unchanged.

shifts.trend: Power Systems: FY25 +16% → Q1 FY26 +19% YoY; Power Generation (DC primary): FY25 +19% → Q1 FY26 +28%
shifts.note: North America and China power generation both strong

shifts.trend: Power Systems EBITDA margin: 22.7% FY25 → 29.5% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Operating leverage, favorable pricing, lower net tariff costs — significant expansion

shifts.trend: Data center backlog 6-8 quarters out in FY25 10-K; Q1 10-Q maintains 'strong demand outlook'
shifts.note: Backlog unchanged in language — no easing signal across two filings

shifts.trend: Engine segment EBITDA -$179M YoY in Q1; medium/heavy truck demand weak H1 2026
shifts.note: Non-power business is a drag; Power is the entire thesis

CMI.verdict: Thesis intact. Power Systems margin expansion to ~30% in Q1 is a positive surprise. Truck weakness is a distraction from the DC power generation story.

COHR.headline: D&C revenue growth re-accelerated from +34% to +41% QoQ with gross margin expanding to 38% (+243bps); Q3 introduced 'scale across' interconnect as a new demand driver on top of existing DCI and AI datacenter.

shifts.trend: D&C YoY: Q2 +34% → Q3 +41%; gross margin: Q2 37% (+140bps) → Q3 38% (+243bps)
shifts.note: Each successive quarter adding new growth category — telecom, DCI, now 'scale across'

shifts.trend: Consolidated gross margin: Q2 37% → Q3 38%; D&C segment profit: $306M → $348M
shifts.note: Yield improvements and pricing optimization in transceivers, plus divestiture mix benefit

shifts.trend: Industrial: Q2 -10% → Q3 -16% on aerospace + Munich divestitures
shifts.note: Portfolio reshaping toward pure AI/datacenter optics — Industrial drag diminishing as divestitures complete

shifts.trend: Interest expense -$13M Q3 YoY, -$39M YTD on term-loan paydown
shifts.note: Balance sheet delevering as earnings grow

COHR.verdict: Thesis strengthening. Re-acceleration in D&C growth, expanding gross margins, and first-ever 'scale across' mention extends the demand narrative beyond current AI cluster buildout.

CRM.headline: Annual revenue grew +10% to $41.5B with operating margin expanding to 20% and RPO jumping to $72.4B; Informatica acquisition closed Nov 2025 to power agentic AI data management.

shifts.note: Consistent but not accelerating — Agentforce consumption pricing still 'relatively new and uncertain'
RPO dipped mid-year then recovered strongly at year-end; cRPO outpacing total is positive

shifts.trend: Operating margin: 19% FY25 → 20% FY26; FCF +15% to $15B
shifts.note: Consistent margin expansion; AI/agent investments not impairing profitability

shifts.trend: Acquired Informatica for $9.6B (Nov 2025) + Agentforce infrastructure investment; $12.7B buybacks FY26
shifts.note: Capital allocation split between M&A (data AI platform) and buybacks — balanced

CRM.verdict: Thesis intact. CRM is executing AI platform integration but growth hasn't yet re-rated to match the Agentforce narrative. The Informatica data layer is the key FY27 watch item.

CRWV.headline: Revenue more than doubled to $2.08B in Q1 2026 (+112% YoY); RPO surged to $98.8B (from $60.7B at year-end) as Meta emerged as a 20% customer alongside Microsoft at 45%; debt raised to ~$21.6B.

shifts.trend: FY25 +168% → Q1 2026 +112% YoY (on much higher base); RPO: $15.1B YE24 → $60.7B YE25 → $98.8B Q1 26
shifts.note: RPO $98.8B is ~47x annualized revenue — extreme forward booking

shifts.trend: Microsoft: 67% FY25 → 45% Q1 26; Meta emerged at 20% Q1 26
shifts.note: Customer base diversifying: OpenAI ($11.9B), Meta ($14.2B), Microsoft remain the three anchors

shifts.trend: Total debt: $8.0B YE24 → $21.6B YE25 → ~$24B post-Q1 capital raise
shifts.note: April 2026: $4B converts + $2.8B senior notes; effective rates 9-15% on senior debt

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $10.3B; Q1 active power 850 MW (vs 360 MW prior year); $1.2B new JV stakes in Q1
shifts.note: Spending well ahead of revenue recognition cadence due to take-or-pay model

CRWV.verdict: Thesis intact but high risk: $98.8B RPO vs $2B quarterly revenue means every dollar matters on execution. Debt load at 15% rates is the existential risk if a hyperscaler renegotiates.

CSCO.headline: Networking revenue growth re-accelerated from +15% to +21% YoY across consecutive quarters; Silicon One hyperscaler order book is building with inventory commitments rising.

shifts.trend: Total: Q1 +8% → Q2 +10% YoY; Networking: Q1 +15% → Q2 +21%
shifts.note: AI Infrastructure the primary driver in both quarters; Service Provider and Cloud leading

shifts.trend: Inventory +7% and purchase commitments +10% at Q1; Silicon One commitments continuing in Q2
shifts.note: CSCO pre-committing Silicon One capacity for hyperscaler orders — raises future obsolescence risk

shifts.trend: Security: -4% in Q2 on Splunk cloud migration headwind
shifts.note: Splunk shift creates near-term Security drag; expected to continue in 2H FY26

shifts.trend: Product GM: -0.6pts Q1 → +0.2pts Q2; net margins improving on lower effective tax rate
shifts.note: Margin stabilizing Q2 after Q1 mix pressure

CSCO.verdict: Thesis intact. CSCO is the second-layer beneficiary of hyperscaler AI buildout (networking + switching). The Silicon One commitment buildup is both a demand signal and a risk of demand concentration.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog jumped to $14.5B (+44% YoY) in Q1 with organic orders +42%; Boyd Thermal acquisition pending for liquid cooling exposure; operating margin compressed 480bps on commodity inflation.

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas: FY25 +16% → Q1 FY26 +20%; backlog +31% YE25 → +44% Q1 FY26 YoY
shifts.note: Data center strength explicitly named as primary driver in both periods

shifts.trend: EA backlog: $13.2B at YE25 → $14.5B Q1 FY26 (+32% organic growth); book-to-bill 1.2x in Q1
shifts.note: Multi-year forward demand building faster than revenue recognition

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas op margin: 29.9% FY25 → 25.6% Q1 FY26 (-480bps)
shifts.note: Commodity and wage inflation headwinds; volume leverage provides 210bps offset — margin dilution worrying at these levels

shifts.trend: Boyd Thermal $9.5B acquisition signed Nov 2025 pending close; Fibrebond + Resilient already closed
shifts.note: M&A push into liquid cooling (Boyd) is the most direct AI thesis play

ETN.verdict: Thesis intact. Backlog build is exceptional but margin contraction to 25.6% in Q1 needs watching — if it persists through the Boyd integration, the unit economics erode the story.

FN.headline: Revenue growth accelerated from +36% to +39% YoY with DCI surging +90%; customer concentration tightened to 4 customers >10% but HPC disclosure ($106.7M) confirms AI infrastructure direct exposure.

shifts.note: DCI and Telecom both accelerating simultaneously
DCI is the fastest-growing optical line — AI scale-across inter-DC fabric ramping

shifts.trend: 4 customers >10% in Q3 (vs 2 at 48.5% prior year); customer consolidation risk on Nokia/Infinera merger
shifts.note: More customers at >10% means broader hyperscaler exposure but Nokia/Infinera M&A creates single-customer risk

shifts.trend: Gross margin: Q2 12.2% → Q3 11.9%; operating margin: Q2 10.1% → Q3 9.9%
shifts.note: Margins stable but compressed from inventory build and Chonburi expansion capex

FN.verdict: Thesis intact. DCI +90% and HPC emergence confirm Fabrinet is a direct AI scale-across beneficiary. Customer concentration is the main risk.

GEV.headline: Total RPO hit $163.3B in Q1 (+32% YoY, +9% QoQ) as heavy-duty gas turbine orders ran at 28 per quarter; Power segment EBITDA margin jumped to 16.3% from 11.6% on favorable pricing.

shifts.trend: Power RPO: $64.9B YE24 → $94.4B YE25 → $99.7B Q1 FY26; Heavy-Duty turbines booked: 41 in 2023 → 68 in 2024 → 110 in 2025; 28 in Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Order velocity sustaining at 2025's elevated pace

shifts.trend: Power segment EBITDA margin: 12.5% FY24 → 14.7% FY25 → 16.3% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Consistent margin expansion on pricing power in a supply-constrained market

shifts.trend: RPO $163.3B vs ~$38B annualized revenue — >4 years of backlog at current run-rate
shifts.note: Supply side can't absorb all demand even on a multi-year horizon

shifts.trend: Prolec GE (transformer JV) acquired Feb 2026 for remaining 50% ($5.3B); Electrification RPO growing
shifts.note: Transformer capacity acquisition addresses upstream bottleneck in the power-to-datacenter chain

GEV.verdict: Thesis intact and possibly the strongest backlog story in the coverage. $163B RPO with expanding margins on favorable pricing — the only question is turbine manufacturing capacity constraints.

GNRC.headline: C&I segment re-accelerated to +28% YoY in Q1 FY26 on data center demand; acquired Enercon for hyperscale custom power; residential-vs-C&I segment reorganization completed.

shifts.trend: FY25: C&I +4.9%, residential -6.8% → Q1 FY26: C&I +28%, residential +1%; total: FY25 -2% → Q1 +12%
shifts.note: Data center is the primary C&I growth driver alongside industrial channels

shifts.trend: Adjusted EBITDA margin: FY25 17.0% → Q1 FY26: residential 25.1% (vs 20.3%), C&I 13.0% (vs 11.4%)
shifts.note: Significant margin improvement both segments — operational efficiency + price/cost

shifts.trend: Large-megawatt DC gensets market described as 'significantly supply-constrained' in FY25; new Enercon acquisition for custom hyperscale power
shifts.note: Supply tightness is structurally positive for pricing and backlog

shifts.trend: FY26: reorganized to Residential/C&I (from Domestic/International); aligns reporting with strategy
shifts.note: Segment change improves visibility into data-center C&I trajectory vs residential cycle

GNRC.verdict: Thesis intact. C&I acceleration and Enercon acquisition extend the DC power story. Residential remains a cyclical drag but DC custom power is now clearly a distinct high-growth category.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud re-accelerated to +63% YoY in Q1 2026; RPO nearly doubled to $467.6B in one quarter on 'multi-gigawatt' TPU supply agreements; capex hit $35.7B in Q1 alone vs $91.4B for all of FY25.

shifts.trend: Cloud YoY: FY25 +36% → Q1 2026 +63%; Cloud op income: $6.1B FY24 → $13.9B FY25 → $6.6B Q1 26 (3x Q1 25)
shifts.note: Cloud growth re-acceleration confirms GOOGL is gaining AI workload share

shifts.trend: Capex: $52.5B FY24 → $91.4B FY25 → $35.7B Q1 2026 alone (pacing $140B+ FY26)
shifts.note: Guided to 'significantly increase' — on track for ~50-60% FY26 growth

shifts.trend: RPO: $242.8B Dec 31 2025 → $467.6B Mar 31 2026 — near-doubled in one quarter on multi-GW TPU hardware supply agreements
shifts.note: First-ever disclosure of multi-GW TPU supply deals is a major demand signal

shifts.trend: $40B private company commitment (Mar 2026) + $31.1B new notes issued Q1; $16B into Waymo
shifts.note: Google investing at extraordinary scale across AI model companies and its own fleet

GOOGL.verdict: Thesis significantly strengthened. The Q1 26 RPO jump to $467B and multi-GW TPU supply agreements represent a structural step-up in AI demand commitment — not just infrastructure spend.

HPE.headline: Juniper fully consolidated drove Networking +151% in Q1; gross margin jumped 670bps on Juniper software mix; ARR +63% to $3.15B but Cloud & AI server segment carrying 'limited margins' on GPU-heavy mix.

shifts.note: Networking step-up from Juniper full-quarter inclusion vs 4-month contribution in prior year

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 32.2% FY25 → 35.9% Q1 FY26 (+670bps); non-GAAP op margin: 9.1% FY25 → 12.7% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Juniper software revenue mix is structurally higher-margin; will normalize as full-year comparisons lap

shifts.trend: Cloud & AI (servers) -2.7% in Q1 FY26; AI servers growing but GPU-heavy orders carry 'limited margins'
shifts.note: HPE competing on AI server TAM but margin profile is structurally disadvantaged vs pure networking

shifts.trend: $600M Juniper synergy target by FY28; $800M integration investment; Catalyst program $350M savings by FY27
shifts.note: Integration is the multi-year margin recovery path; near-term costs still elevated

HPE.verdict: Thesis mixed: Networking/Juniper story is real and high-quality; Server/Cloud & AI is a volume play with thin margins. Watch for Juniper synergy execution in FY26-27.

HUBB.headline: Electrical Solutions growth accelerated to organic +10.6% in Q1 on continued datacenter strength; total backlog $2.16B at YE25 (all expected in 2026); but Electrical Solutions margin contracted 30bps on inflation.

shifts.trend: Electrical Solutions: FY25 +7.1% organic → Q1 FY26 +10.6% organic
shifts.note: Datacenter vertical consistently named as primary growth driver

shifts.trend: Electrical Solutions adj op margin: 20.2% FY25 → 16.4% Q1 FY26 (-30bps YoY in Q1); overall adj op margin +110bps Q1
shifts.note: Overall improving but Electrical Solutions margin slightly pressured by commodity/tariff inflation

shifts.trend: Backlog: $1.90B YE24 → $2.16B YE25 → expected to ship substantially all in 2026
shifts.note: Backlog growth is positive but relatively modest vs peers like ETN/PWR

shifts.trend: $958M acquisitions in 2025 to expand product reach in high-growth markets
shifts.note: Inorganic growth complementing organic datacenter and T&D demand

HUBB.verdict: Thesis intact. HUBB is a smaller-scale version of ETN/PWR for datacenter electrical solutions. Organic +10.6% with multi-year backlog confirms demand but not at the same scale.

INTC.headline: DCAI revenue re-accelerated to +22% in Q1 on supply-constrained premium-mix pricing; Intel Foundry external revenue remains minimal ($174M) despite 18A high-volume ramp; strategic partnerships (NVIDIA $5B, SoftBank $2B) suggest external validation.

shifts.note: Supply constraints limiting volume but driving ASP premium — counterintuitively bullish for margins

shifts.trend: Intel 7 supply constraints: severe Q4 25 → persist H1 2026; substrates and memory shortages added in Q1 filing
shifts.note: Supply tightening is both a risk and a pricing tailwind; same supply note two filings in a row

shifts.trend: Gross margin: FY25 ~39% → Q1 2026 39.4% (+2.5pts YoY); DCAI ASPs +27% on premium mix
shifts.note: Gross margin improving slowly despite $4.1B Q1 restructuring charges

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry op loss: $13.3B FY24 → $10.3B FY25 → $2.4B Q1 2026 alone (on track for ~$10B FY26)
shifts.note: External foundry revenue minimal ($307M FY25, $174M Q1 26); breakeven still years away

INTC.verdict: Thesis precarious: NVIDIA $5B partnership and SoftBank $2B are external validations of Intel 18A viability, but Intel Foundry is still burning $2.4B per quarter with $174M external revenue. DCAI supply premium is a tailwind, not a moat.

KLAC.headline: Record Q3 revenue $3.42B (+11% YoY); Korea +80% on HBM memory investment; China declining to 24% from 30% as export controls bite; gross margin dipped 50bps on DRAM chip cost pressure framed as transitory.

shifts.note: HBM/DRAM memory investment ramping strongly in Korea; Taiwan -12% on shipment timing is noise

shifts.trend: China: Q2 30.2% ($995M, -9% YoY) → Q3 24.3% ($830M, +5% but declining share)
shifts.note: 9-month China flat at $3.09B — US export controls capping growth; structural decline in share

shifts.trend: Gross margin: Q2 61.4% (+1.1pts YoY) → Q3 61.1% (-0.5pts QoQ); DRAM chip cost headwind framed as transitory
shifts.note: GM headwind is specific to image-computer DRAM cost input — not customer pricing pressure

shifts.trend: Q2: $548M repurchases + 16th consecutive dividend raise → Q3: $626M repurchases + $7B new authorization + 17th consecutive raise
shifts.note: Capital return program accelerating even at record revenue levels — management confidence signal

KLAC.verdict: Thesis intact. KLAC is benefiting from HBM-driven process-control intensity; China headwind is a known risk. Record Q3 and Korea +80% confirm the demand is real and growing.

LEU.headline: Jan 2026: $900M DOE HALEU task order won; backlog grew to $3.9B with $2.4B contingent; signed Fluor EPC contract for Piketon expansion; exploring Oklo JV for HALEU deconversion at Pike County campus.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $308M → Q1 FY26 $76.7M (+5% YoY); backlog: $3.7B YE24 → $3.8B YE25 → $3.9B Q1 26
shifts.note: Revenue relatively steady; growth is in backlog and contract pipeline (contingent)

shifts.trend: SWU spot price $200 vs $34 historic low (2018); LEU is only NRC-licensed active HALEU enricher
shifts.note: Supply concentration is structural: Russia still 43% of global capacity; non-Russian undersupplied

shifts.trend: Oak Ridge centrifuge manufacturing started Dec 2025 ($560M+ multi-year); Piketon EPC signed Feb 2026; Oklo JV announced
shifts.note: Multiple simultaneous capital projects — rapid expansion from single-site demo to commercial scale

shifts.trend: $900M DOE task order won Jan 2026; Aurora-INL pathway granted; NRC Phase I pre-application with no significant gaps
shifts.note: Government-to-commercial bridge is materializing faster than expected

LEU.verdict: Thesis intact. LEU is executing the government-supported nuclear fuel supply buildout in real time. The Oklo JV adds a vertical integration angle (fuel + reactor) that strengthens the moat.

LITE.headline: Revenue surged to +90% YoY in Q3 with gross margin expanding 1,540bps to 44.2%; supply allocation language hardened and top customer share rose from 24% to 26% — LITE is the 1.6T transceiver choke point.

shifts.note: Re-acceleration from an already extraordinary growth rate — demand outpacing supply
Supply allocation + 200G lane-speed mix is re-rating the margin structure. If sustained, structurally different business.

shifts.trend: Q2: 'demand outpacing supply, has led to allocation decisions' → Q3: language hardened to 'has required us to make decisions'
shifts.note: Active supply allocation in place and language intensifying — hard evidence of bottleneck status

shifts.trend: Q2: top 2 customers 24% + 17% → Q3: 26% + 12%
shifts.note: Top customer share rising; concentration tightening as hyperscaler demand dominates

LITE.verdict: Thesis strongly confirmed. LITE's 200G InP/EML chip supply allocation and 44% gross margin are the clearest bottleneck signal in the optics layer. Story could break if LITE adds capacity and allocation ends.

LRCX.headline: Revenue grew 9% QoQ in Q3 as DRAM investment surged; memory mix shifted from 34% to 39% while foundry declined from 59% to 54%; China held at 34% of revenue — flat QoQ after declining prior quarter.

shifts.trend: Q2 revenue flat QoQ; Q3 +9% QoQ; 9M revenue +24% YoY
shifts.note: DRAM customer investment surge drove sequential acceleration

shifts.trend: Memory mix: 34% Q2 → 39% Q3; Foundry: 59% Q2 → 54% Q3; mature node decline offsetting logic
shifts.note: HBM-driven DRAM investment is the demand driver; foundry weakness from mature nodes, not leading edge

shifts.trend: China: Q2 35% → Q3 34% of revenue; flat in absolute dollars
shifts.note: China stabilizing after decline from 43% in Q1 FY26 — export controls have set a new floor

shifts.trend: Gross margin: Q2 49.6% → Q3 49.8% (+0.2pts)
shifts.note: Stable margins despite mix shift; installed base customer support revenue growing

LRCX.verdict: Thesis intact. DRAM/HBM investment cycle is the growth driver. China plateau suggests export controls have been absorbed without further disruption. The 9% QoQ step in Q3 is a positive inflection.

META.headline: Capex guidance raised to $125-145B (from $115-135B) for 2026 and Q1 26 revenue grew +33%; non-cancelable commitments hit $237.7B with an additional $24B added in April — META is becoming the infrastructure anchor for multiple AI model companies.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: FY25 +22% → Q1 2026 +33%; ad impressions: +12% FY25 → +19% Q1 26; price per ad: +9% → +12%
shifts.note: Both volume and pricing accelerating simultaneously — advertising model strengthening

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $72.2B → FY26 guidance $115-135B (Jan) → $125-145B (Apr); Q1 26 alone $19.8B
shifts.note: Guidance raised $10B in one quarter; management signaling AI is 'no ceiling' priority

shifts.trend: Non-cancelable commitments: $237.7B at Mar 31 + $24B more added Apr 2026; off-balance-sheet leases not yet commenced $182.9B
shifts.note: Total AI infrastructure commitment likely >$400B when all obligations tallied

shifts.trend: Operating income: $83.3B FY25 (41% margin) → $22.9B Q1 26 (40.7% margin)
shifts.note: Capex acceleration not yet showing in operating margins — advertising profitability holding

META.verdict: META is the biggest hyperscaler capex growth story in absolute dollars: $125-145B in 2026 vs $72B last year. Primary demand signal for the entire AI infrastructure stack.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data center revenue accelerated from +$67M in Q2 to +$130M QoQ in Q3 (+51% YoY); Reverse Morris Trust announced Jan 2026 to become a pure-play data center + HVAC company.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions: Q2 +24% YoY → Q3 +51% YoY; data center YoY dollar adds: Q2 +$67.4M → Q3 +$130.0M
shifts.note: Re-acceleration in data center cooling as US capacity ramp matures

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions op margin: Q2 13.7% (vs 17.6% prior year) → Q3 15.3% (vs 17.3%) — still pressured but recovering
shifts.note: Temporary inefficiencies from rapid US manufacturing expansion; recovering quarter-over-quarter

shifts.trend: Performance Technologies -4% Q2 → offset by Climate Solutions; Reverse Morris Trust announced to separate
shifts.note: RMT with Gentherm (value ~$1B) creates a pure-play Climate Solutions entity; significant strategic clarity

shifts.trend: Incremental $100M DC capex announced Q2; new DC manufacturing lease signed Q3; FY26 capex $59.4M 1H
shifts.note: Capex specifically for US data center cooling manufacturing capacity

MOD.verdict: Thesis evolving positively: the RMT creates a pure-play data center cooling vehicle. Revenue re-acceleration to +51% confirms demand. Margin recovery to prior levels is the key watch metric.

MRVL.headline: Data center reached 74% of FY26 revenue at $6.1B (+46% YoY); custom AI ASIC platform (5nm → 3nm → 2nm roadmap) now explicit; Celestial AI and XConn acquired post-year-end for photonic fabric and CXL/PCIe switching.

shifts.trend: Q3 FY26: DC +38% → FY26 annual: DC +46%; total: Q3 +37% → FY26 +42%
shifts.note: Accelerating from already-strong Q3 base — custom ASIC ramp continuing

shifts.trend: Gross margin: Q3 FY26 51.6% → FY26 51.0% (vs 41.3% prior year — prior year had $358M restructuring impairments)
shifts.note: Margins normalizing at ~51% post-restructuring; no further expansion in FY26 vs Q3

shifts.trend: Direct customer A: 11% Q3 → 14% FY26; Distributor A: 34% Q3 → 37% FY26
shifts.note: One hyperscaler ASIC customer increasingly dominant; Distributor concentration at 37% is extreme

shifts.trend: Celestial AI ~$1.3B cash + shares (Feb 2026); XConn $280M + shares (Feb 2026) — both post-FY26-close
shifts.note: Expanding into photonic fabric (Celestial) and CXL/PCIe switching (XConn) to address AI scale-up interconnect

MRVL.verdict: Thesis intact and expanding. DC at 74% of revenue with explicit 2nm ASIC roadmap and photonic fabric M&A positions MRVL as the custom AI compute fabric company. Concentration risk at one hyperscaler is the primary risk.

MSFT.headline: Azure growth re-accelerated from +39% to +40%; commercial RPO sustained near $625-627B range (+99-110% YoY); quarterly capex held at $29-31B range per quarter as AI infrastructure spend continued.

shifts.trend: Azure YoY: Q2 +39% → Q3 +40%; Microsoft Cloud: Q2 +26% → Q3 +29%
shifts.note: Slight acceleration in both periods; 9M Cloud revenue $155.1B (+27%)

shifts.trend: Q2 capex $29.9B → Q3 capex $30.9B; 9M total $80.1B (vs $40.9B prior year)
shifts.note: Run-rate of ~$120B+ annualized; essentially steady at peak AI infra investment

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO: $625B Q2 → $627B Q3 (+99% YoY at Q3); ~25% to recognize in next 12 months
shifts.note: RPO growth plateauing near $625B — still enormous but growth rate decelerating from Q2's 110%

shifts.trend: MS Cloud gross margin: 67% Q2 → consistent trend; operating income +20-21% in both quarters
shifts.note: Consistent margin profile; $5.9B OpenAI Recapitalization gain (Q3 non-operating) is non-recurring

MSFT.verdict: Thesis intact. Azure acceleration to +40% with $80B 9M capex confirms MSFT as the primary hyperscaler AI platform. RPO plateau near $627B is the one thing to watch.

MXL.headline: Infrastructure segment re-accelerated to +36.3M YoY in Q1 as optical DSPs ramp; customer concentration broadened (top customer down from >20% to 13%); revenue trajectory from FY25 +30% continues into Q1 +43%.

shifts.note: Infrastructure +$36.3M in Q1 on optical DSP and wireless backhaul/access growth

shifts.trend: Top customer: >20% historical → 13% Q1 26; top-10: 65% FY25 → 56% Q1 26
shifts.note: Broadening customer base suggests optical DSP winning with multiple customers

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 57% FY25 (+3pts) → Q1 26 trend consistent
shifts.note: Margin improvement from mix shift to higher-value optical/infrastructure products

shifts.trend: 800G to 1.6T optical DSP transition explicitly called the next-leg opportunity
shifts.note: FY25 10-K provides the most explicit 1.6T roadmap framing in the coverage for optical DSPs

MXL.verdict: Thesis intact. MXL's optical DSP business is riding the 1.6T switch cycle. Concentrated-to-broadening customer base is a positive. Size ($137M quarterly revenue) makes it leveraged to optical but small.

NOW.headline: Revenue grew +22% consistently in both periods; RPO held at ~$27-28B with cRPO +23-25%; Now Assist AI agent adoption consistent but consumption pricing model still described as 'relatively new' — steady compounding, not step-change.

shifts.trend: FY25 +21% → Q1 2026 +22% YoY; subscription +21% → +22%
shifts.note: Consistent and steady; AI agent consumption pricing not yet moving the needle materially

shifts.trend: Total RPO: $28.2B FY25 → $27.7B Q1 26 (+25% YoY at Q1); cRPO 46% of RPO in both periods
shifts.note: RPO slightly down QoQ but YoY growth strong; consistent backlog profile

shifts.trend: Self-hosted upfront: $492M FY25 → $138M Q1 26 (vs $157M Q1 25 — slight decline)
shifts.note: Government/self-hosted growth moderating; mostly commercial cloud mix

shifts.trend: Subscription gross margin slightly pressured by self-hosted mix in Q1; operating leverage intact
shifts.note: No material change in margin profile across the two periods

NOW.verdict: Thesis intact but not accelerating. NOW is compounding steadily at 22% with Now Assist deep in the product suite. The AI agent monetization story needs a quarterly proof point to unlock a higher multiple.

NVT.headline: Systems Protection organic growth re-accelerated to +50% in Q1 FY26 (vs +17% FY25 organic) on 46-point infrastructure/data center contribution; gross margin compressed 290bps on tariffs and capacity investment.

shifts.trend: Systems Protection organic: FY25 +17% → Q1 FY26 +50.1%; total: FY25 +13% organic → Q1 FY26 +34.4% organic
shifts.note: Dramatic re-acceleration — EPG acquisition contributing ~$121M to Q1 Systems Protection

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 37.7% FY25 → 35.9% Q1 FY26 (-290bps); Systems Protection operating margin: 20.7% FY25 → 22.7% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Consolidated GM compressed by tariffs but Systems Protection segment margin expanding on volume leverage

shifts.trend: Backlog $2.3B at YE25 (firm orders); data center described as 'primary driver' of backlog build
shifts.note: Backlog context modest vs ETN/VRT but growing

shifts.trend: FY26 capex guided to $425-525M (vs $226.4M in FY25); EPG acquisition $1.0B (May 2025)
shifts.note: Capex doubling to support data center enclosure and power capacity

NVT.verdict: Thesis intact. The Q1 acceleration to +50% organic Systems Protection confirms the hyperscaler enclosure story is real. Tariff-driven gross margin contraction is the watch item.

OKLO.headline: Order book reached ~18,100 MWe and Meta signed a $1.2 GW prepayment agreement in Jan 2026 for Pike County, Ohio — first hyperscaler prepayment for advanced fission; FY26 capex guided $350-450M.

shifts.trend: Order book: ~18,100 MWe (+2,500% since 2023); Meta 1.2 GW prepayment signed Jan 2026
shifts.note: Meta prepayment is the first commercial hyperscaler advanced-fission contract with cash upfront

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $605.9M (investing activities) → FY26 guided $350-450M; ATM raised $968M FY25 + $300M Dec 2025
shifts.note: Capital raised ahead of construction timeline; FY26 capex heavy on Aurora development

shifts.trend: NRC Phase I pre-application: no significant gaps (Jul 2025) → Aurora-INL DOE authorization approved (Feb 2026)
shifts.note: Regulatory milestones accelerating vs historical nuclear development timelines

shifts.trend: Order book: Switch 12 GW master agreement + Meta 1.2 GW prepay + Equinix, Diamondback, Prometheus LOIs
shifts.note: Customer diversification from original Switch relationship to multiple hyperscalers and industrial

OKLO.verdict: Thesis high-conviction speculative. The Meta prepayment is a genuine commercial milestone — first real cash from a hyperscaler for advanced fission. FY26 execution on Aurora is the thesis-test event.

ORCL.headline: OCI cloud infrastructure growth re-accelerated from +68% to +84% YoY; RPO hit $552.6B (4.2x YoY) and 9-month capex was $39.2B (3.2x prior year); $42.7B of senior notes + $5B mandatory convert issued to fund the build.

shifts.trend: OCI cloud infrastructure YoY: Q2 +68% → Q3 +84%; total revenue: Q2 +14% → Q3 +22%
shifts.note: OCI acceleration while total revenue also re-accelerated — AI workloads the driver

shifts.trend: H1 capex $20.5B → 9M capex $39.2B (Q3 added ~$18.7B); 3.2x prior year 9M
shifts.note: Capex run-rate of ~$52B annualized — extraordinary ramp from ~$6B prior year

shifts.trend: RPO: $97.3B Nov 24 → $523.3B Nov 25 → $552.6B Feb 26; 5.4x YoY at Q2, 4.2x YoY at Q3
shifts.note: RPO growth decelerating slightly (5.4x → 4.2x) as base laps last year's contract burst

shifts.trend: Issued $17.9B notes (Sep 25) + $25B notes (Feb 26) + $5B mandatory convert; total debt expanding rapidly
shifts.note: ORCL is debt-financing the AI capex ramp — interest burden increasing

ORCL.verdict: Thesis intact but watch leverage. OCI +84% and $552B RPO are extraordinary demand signals. But $42.7B of debt issuance in 6 months means the bet on cloud AI ramp is enormous — no margin for revenue miss.

PLTR.headline: Revenue growth re-accelerated sharply to +85% YoY in Q1 2026 (from +53% FY25); operating margin expanded to 46% (vs 20%) on contribution margin discipline; commercial re-overtook government in growth velocity.

shifts.trend: Total: FY25 +53% → Q1 2026 +85%; commercial: FY25 +60% → Q1 26 +95%; government: FY25 +53% → Q1 26 +76%
shifts.note: Extraordinary acceleration across both segments — AI infrastructure platform adoption inflecting

shifts.trend: Operating margin: FY25 31% → Q1 2026 46%; gross margin: Q1 25 80% → Q1 26 87%
shifts.note: Margin expansion on revenue growth without proportional cost increase — operating leverage is real

shifts.trend: No customer >10% in Q1 26 or Q1 25 (vs potential single-customer concentration in early years)
shifts.note: Improved diversification signals AIP/Foundry/Gotham adoption beyond original large-contract model

shifts.trend: US commercial +95%; US government +76%; international trailing both (26% of revenue)
shifts.note: US the primary growth engine; international lag may be next growth unlock

PLTR.verdict: Thesis strongly confirmed. +85% growth with 46% operating margin is exceptional quality of earnings. The story has shifted from 'when does it scale' to 'how fast is it scaling'.

PWR.headline: Q1 backlog reached $48.47B (+10% QoQ) and RPO $26.24B (+10% QoQ); revenue grew +26% with operating income +42%; data center named as primary catalyst across both Electric and Underground segments.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 +20% → Q1 FY26 +26% YoY; operating income: FY25 +18% → Q1 FY26 +42%
shifts.note: Accelerating top-line with significant operating leverage improvement

shifts.trend: RPO: $23.76B YE25 → $26.24B Q1 FY26 (+10% in one quarter); backlog: $43.98B → $48.47B (+10% QoQ)
shifts.note: Both RPO and backlog growing at double-digit rates sequentially — demand pipeline still accelerating

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 13.4% FY25 → 14.1% Q1 FY26; operating margin: 4.1% → 4.3%
shifts.note: Modest but consistent improvement in execution quality

shifts.trend: FY25 $3.30B on acquisitions; Q1 26 SG&A +$127M from $51M acquisitions + $62M compensation
shifts.note: Integration of DSI (Underground) and CEI still adding to cost base

PWR.verdict: Thesis intact. Backlog compounding at 10% per quarter while revenue grows at 26% confirms the data-center-driven electrical infrastructure buildout has 3-5 years of runway.

SNOW.headline: Product revenue grew +31% to $4.5B FY26 with RPO expanding from $7.9B to $9.8B; NRR held at 125% with 733 $1M+ customers (vs 576); consumption model growth is steady but not accelerating.

shifts.trend: Product revenue: Q3 +29% → FY26 +31% YoY
shifts.note: Consistent ~30% growth rate; AI/data workload consumption steadily driving it

shifts.trend: RPO: $7.9B Oct 31 25 → $9.8B Jan 31 26 (+24% QoQ growth); 46% recognized next 12 months
shifts.note: RPO grew meaningfully in Q4 after flat trend mid-year

shifts.trend: Net revenue retention: 126% (Jan 25) → 125% (Oct 25) → 125% (Jan 26)
shifts.note: NRR stable — slight moderation but no material deterioration

shifts.trend: SBC as % of revenue: 41% FY25 → 34% FY26; operating cash flow $1.2B FY26
shifts.note: Cost structure improving as revenue scales faster than headcount

SNOW.verdict: Thesis intact at steady growth. SNOW is compounding at 30% with improving cost structure. The next re-rating needs evidence that AI workloads are accelerating the consumption model above 30%.

SNPS.headline: Q1 FY26 revenue +66% YoY on Ansys consolidation; Design IP -6% (structural weakness) and Design Automation +96% (Ansys-driven); $3.5B term loan paid off in Q1; Processor IP sold to GFS.

shifts.trend: Total: FY25 +15% → Q1 FY26 +66% YoY (Ansys close); Design Automation: FY25 organic moderate → Q1 FY26 +96%
shifts.note: Ansys contributed $885.6M of Q1 growth; organic Design Automation also strong

shifts.trend: China: FY25 -22% ex-Ansys → Q1 FY26 'challenging near-term' language maintained
shifts.note: BIS controls resolved for EDA tools but macro still soft; China structural headwind remains

shifts.trend: Design IP: FY25 muted growth flagged → Q1 FY26 -6% YoY; Processor IP sold to GFS
shifts.note: Design IP weakness persisting; portfolio pruning toward highest-growth opportunities

shifts.trend: $13.5B total debt at FY25 end → $3.5B term loan paid off Q1 FY26; buyback still suspended
shifts.note: Aggressive debt paydown; buyback resumption (and re-rating catalyst) closer with each quarter

SNPS.verdict: Thesis intact but the next 2 quarters will define it: debt paydown is on track, but Design IP weakness persists. Ansys integration execution and Design Automation organic growth need to hold above 15%+ to justify the $35B acquisition.

VRT.headline: Systems Protection organic growth re-accelerated to +50% in Q1 FY26 (vs ~+17% FY25 organic) with gross margin expanding 400bps to 37.7%; backlog doubled YoY to $15B; capex guided to double.

shifts.trend: Americas: FY25 +42% → Q1 FY26 +53% YoY; total organic: ~13% FY25 → 34.4% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Organic acceleration dominates — acquisition contribution is additive to already-strong organic

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 36.3% FY25 → 37.7% Q1 FY26 (+400bps YoY); Americas op margin: 24.4% FY25 → 27.0% Q1
shifts.note: Volume leverage and mix driving consistent margin expansion — running above full-year target

shifts.trend: Combined order backlog: $7.2B YE24 → $15.0B YE25 — more than doubled; expected to ship in next 12-18 months
shifts.note: Backlog doubling is the clearest supply-tightness signal; mgmt cites 'very robust' AI/HPC demand

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $226.4M → FY26 guided $425-525M (2x); Americas Americas driving expansion
shifts.note: Doubling capex while margins expand — rare combination that signals high returns on incremental capacity

VRT.verdict: VRT is the strongest quality-of-earnings story in the power/cooling stack: organic +34%, margins expanding, backlog doubled, capex doubling. Thesis fully intact and possibly the best risk/reward in the coverage.
